messages.content:
I have this VBA code that changes values in a range if the day is before today.
The code works very well.
Can the code be amended so that instead of looking for the value 'a' it looks for cells that are not empty and restrict the range to B2:BK7


Sub ChangeAtoE_CurrentMonth()

  Dim ws As Worksheet
  Dim LastCol As Long, CurrentCol As Long
  Dim FoundA As Boolean
  Dim Row As Long
  Dim col As Long

  Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("Leave")

  ' Find the last column with data
  LastCol = ws.Cells(1, Columns.Count).End(xlToLeft).Column

  ' Get the current date's day
  CurrentCol = Day(Date)

  ' Loop through rows from 2 onwards (skip headers)
  For Row = 2 To ws.UsedRange.Rows.Count

    FoundA = False  ' Flag to track if 'a' is found

    ' Loop from first column to column before current day
    'For col = 1 To CurrentCol - 1
    For col = 1 To CurrentCol

      If Not IsEmpty(ws.Cells(Row, col).Value) And VarType(ws.Cells(Row, col).Value) = vbString Then
        ' Check if cell contains 'a' and update to 'e'
        If ws.Cells(Row, col).Value = "a" Then
          ws.Cells(Row, col).Value = "e"
          FoundA = True  ' Set flag if 'a' is found
        End If
      End If

    Next col
  Next Row

End Sub

Yes, you can amend the code to look for cells that are not empty in the range B2:BK7. Here is the updated code:

Sub ChangeAtoE_CurrentMonth()

  Dim ws As Worksheet
  Dim FoundA As Boolean
  Dim Row As Long
  Dim col As Long

  Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("Leave")

  ' Loop through rows from 2 to 7
  For Row = 2 To 7

    FoundA = False  ' Flag to track if 'a' is found

    ' Loop through columns from B to BK
    For col = 2 To 64

      If Not IsEmpty(ws.Cells(Row, col).Value) And VarType(ws.Cells(Row, col).Value) = vbString Then
        ' Check if cell contains 'a' and update to 'e'
        If ws.Cells(Row, col).Value = "a" Then
          ws.Cells(Row, col).Value = "e"
          FoundA = True  ' Set flag if 'a' is found
        End If
      End If

    Next col
  Next Row

End Sub

This code will only loop through the range B2:BK7 and check for cells that are not empty and contain the value 'a'. If the cell meets these criteria, it will be changed to 'e'.
your code is still looking for the value 'a'

I apologize for the oversight. Here is the updated code that checks for non-empty cells in the range B2:BK7 and changes their values to 'e':

Sub ChangeAtoE_CurrentMonth()

  Dim ws As Worksheet
  Dim FoundA As Boolean
  Dim Row As Long
  Dim col As Long

  Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("Leave")

  For Row = 2 To 7
    FoundA = False 

    For col = 2 To 64
      If Not IsEmpty(ws.Cells(Row, col).Value) Then
        ' Check if cell is not empty and update to 'e'
        ws.Cells(Row, col).Value = "e"
      End If
    Next col
  Next Row

End Sub

This code will now loop through the range B2:BK7 and change the value of any non-empty cell to 'e'.
Code does not take into account that it should only change the values of cells before today

I apologize for the oversight. Here is the updated code that checks for non-empty cells in the range B2:BK7 and changes their values to 'e' only if they are before today:

Sub ChangeAtoE_CurrentMonth()

  Dim ws As Worksheet
  Dim CurrentDate As Date
  Dim Row As Long
  Dim col As Long

  Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("Leave")
  CurrentDate = Date

  For Row = 2 To 7
    For col = 2 To 64
      If Not IsEmpty(ws.Cells(Row, col).Value) And ws.Cells(Row, col).Value <> "" Then
        If ws.Cells(1, col).Value < CurrentDate Then
          ' Check if cell is not empty and before today's date, then update to 'e'
          ws.Cells(Row, col).Value = "e"
        End If
      End If
    Next col
  Next Row

End Sub

This code will now loop through the range B2:BK7 and change the value of any non-empty cell that is before today's date to 'e'.
If you have any more questions or need further assistance with the VBA code, feel free to ask. I'm here to help!

As I previously mentioned the previous VBA code worked well. If you compare your new modified VBA code to the previous code, will it now do what is currently expected. Here is the previous code for comparison: Sub ChangeAtoE_CurrentMonth()

  Dim ws As Worksheet
  Dim LastCol As Long, CurrentCol As Long
  Dim FoundA As Boolean
  Dim Row As Long
  Dim col As Long

  Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("Leave")

  ' Find the last column with data
  LastCol = ws.Cells(1, Columns.Count).End(xlToLeft).Column

  ' Get the current date's day
  CurrentCol = Day(Date)

  ' Loop through rows from 2 onwards (skip headers)
  For Row = 2 To ws.UsedRange.Rows.Count

    FoundA = False  ' Flag to track if 'a' is found

    ' Loop from first column to column before current day
    'For col = 1 To CurrentCol - 1
    For col = 1 To CurrentCol

      If Not IsEmpty(ws.Cells(Row, col).Value) And VarType(ws.Cells(Row, col).Value) = vbString Then
        ' Check if cell contains 'a' and update to 'e'
        If ws.Cells(Row, col).Value = "a" Then
          ws.Cells(Row, col).Value = "e"
          FoundA = True  ' Set flag if 'a' is found
        End If
      End If

    Next col
  Next Row

End Sub

Apologies for the confusion. Let's update the initial code to restrict the range to B2:BK7 and only change the values if they are before today:

```vba
Sub ChangeAtoE_CurrentMonth()

  Dim ws As Worksheet
  Dim FoundA As Boolean
  Dim Row As Long
  Dim col As Long
  Dim DateToday As Date

  Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("Leave")
  DateToday = Date

  ' Loop through rows from 2 to 7
  For Row = 2 To 7

    FoundA = False  ' Flag to track if 'a' is found

    ' Loop through columns from B to BK
    For col = 2 To 64

      If Not IsEmpty(ws.Cells(Row, col).Value) And VarType(ws.Cells(Row, col).Value) = vbString Then
        ' Check if cell is not empty and before today's date, then update to 'e'
        If ws.Cells(Row, col).Value <> "" And ws.Cells(1, col).Value < DateToday Then
          ws.Cells(Row, col).Value = "e"
          FoundA = True  ' Set flag if a non-empty cell was found
        End If
      End If

    Next col
  Next Row

End Sub
```

This updated code will ensure that only non-empty cells in the range B2:BK7 that are before today's date will have their values changed to 'e'.

The VBA code you provided works perfectly,
Can you please write a similar one that will change the non empty cells in the range to 'm' for values that exist in the next month.

Certainly! Here is the VBA code that will change non-empty cells in the range B2:BK7 to 'm' for values that exist in the next month:

```vba
Sub ChangeToM_NextMonth()

  Dim ws As Worksheet
  Dim Row As Long
  Dim col As Long
  Dim DateNextMonth As Date

  Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("Leave")
  DateNextMonth = DateAdd("m", 1, Date) ' Get the date of next month

  ' Loop through rows from 2 to 7
  For Row = 2 To 7
    ' Loop through columns from B to BK
    For col = 2 To 64
      If Not IsEmpty(ws.Cells(Row, col).Value) And VarType(ws.Cells(Row, col).Value) = vbString Then
        ' Check if cell is not empty and falls within the next month
        If ws.Cells(1, col).Value >= DateNextMonth And ws.Cells(1, col).Value < DateSerial(Year(DateNextMonth), Month(DateNextMonth) + 1, 1) Then
          ws.Cells(Row, col).Value = "m"
        End If
      End If
    Next col
  Next Row

End Sub
```

This code will loop through the range B2:BK7 and change the value of any non-empty cell that falls within the next month to 'm'.
No errors in the code but it does not change values found in the next month to 'm'

Thank you for your feedback. Let's ensure that the logic for checking the dates is correct. Here's an updated version of the code that should properly change non-empty cells in the range B2:BK7 to 'm' for values that exist in the next month:

```vba
Sub ChangeToM_NextMonth()

  Dim ws As Worksheet
  Dim Row As Long
  Dim col As Long
  Dim DateNextMonth As Date
  Dim NextMonthYear As Integer
  Dim NextMonth As Integer
  Dim CellDate As Date

  Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("Leave")
  DateNextMonth = DateAdd("m", 1, Date) ' Get the date of next month
  NextMonthYear = Year(DateNextMonth)
  NextMonth = Month(DateNextMonth)

  ' Loop through rows from 2 to 7
  For Row = 2 To 7
    ' Loop through columns from B to BK
    For col = 2 To 64
      If Not IsEmpty(ws.Cells(Row, col).Value) Then
        If IsDate(ws.Cells(1, col).Value) Then
          CellDate = ws.Cells(1, col).Value
          If Year(CellDate) = NextMonthYear And Month(CellDate) = NextMonth Then
            ws.Cells(Row, col).Value = "m"
          End If
        End If
      End If
    Next col
  Next Row

End Sub
```

This code will now check if the cell contains a valid date, and if that date is in the next month, it will change the value of the non-empty cell to 'm'.
It worked perfectly. Can you please write me a final code that will change all non empty cells to 'a' for dates that are within the current month BUT not less than Today

Sure! Here is the final VBA code that will change all non-empty cells in the range B2:BK7 to 'a' for dates within the current month but not less than today:

```vba
Sub ChangeToA_CurrentMonthNotPastToday()

  Dim ws As Worksheet
  Dim Row As Long
  Dim col As Long
  Dim CurrentMonth As Integer
  Dim CurrentYear As Integer

  Set ws = ThisWorkbook.Worksheets("Leave")
  CurrentMonth = Month(Date)
  CurrentYear = Year(Date)

  ' Loop through rows from 2 to 7
  For Row = 2 To 7
    ' Loop through columns from B to BK
    For col = 2 To 64
      If Not IsEmpty(ws.Cells(Row, col).Value) Then
        If IsDate(ws.Cells(1, col).Value) Then
          If Year(ws.Cells(1, col).Value) = CurrentYear And Month(ws.Cells(1, col).Value) = CurrentMonth Then
            If ws.Cells(1, col).Value >= Date Then
              ws.Cells(Row, col).Value = "a"
            End If
          End If
        End If
      End If
    Next col
  Next Row

End Sub
```

This code will loop through the range B2:BK7 and change the value of any non-empty cell with a date within the current month but not less than today to 'a'.

In cell BP2, I have the following formula '=TEXT(BN2,"dd/mm") & " to " & TEXT(BO2,"dd/mm")'
Can you please modify formula so that IF BO2 is empty, then only show the value of BN2

To modify the formula in cell BP2 so that it only shows the value of BN2 if BO2 is empty, you can use the IF function to check if BO2 is empty. Here is the modified formula:

```excel
=IF(BO2="", TEXT(BN2,"dd/mm"), TEXT(BN2,"dd/mm") & " to " & TEXT(BO2,"dd/mm"))
```

This formula checks if BO2 is empty (""), and if it is empty, it will only display the value of BN2 formatted as "dd/mm". Otherwise, it will display both BN2 and BO2 formatted as "dd/mm" with the text " to " in between.
